expectations.text: [defect_replay][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: missing scenario generation for template-save and report-builder loading
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the benchmark focus is explicitly stated and then addressed with scenario patches for both areas: (1) template-save/save-as overwrite: “Missing scenario generation for template-save…” plus scenarios under “Save / Save As / Template Save” including “Save-As: overwrite existing report…” and “Create from template: Save creates a new report instance…”. (2) report-builder loading: “Missing scenario generation for report-builder loading…” plus scenarios under “Prompt handling + Report Builder loading (double-click / edit cycles)” including “Report Builder: prompt elements load and remain interactive after double-click…”.

expectations.text: [defect_replay][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md repeatedly asserts Phase 4a alignment and uses Phase 4a structure: “Primary phase under test: phase4a (subcategory-only draft writer)” and “Evidence-backed Phase 4a scenario patches (subcategory-only)”. It also claims compliance with Phase 4a style: “They are Phase 4a compliant: subcategory → scenario → atomic action chain → observable verification leaves; no canonical categories.” The scenarios are presented as subcategory sections with nested action chains and observable checks, consistent with that description.
